summary: klaudius ships hardcoded Telegram C2 exfiltration endpoint
details: The package's bundled output (dist/bin.js and dist/chunk-SZ4KCTSL.js) contains hardcoded fetch/POST calls to https://api.telegram.org, the canonical Telegram Bot API used as a C2/exfiltration channel by malicious npm packages. The Telegram bot endpoint pattern (api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage) is a well-documented exfiltration sink because the attacker only needs a bot token to receive messages from any installer running the package. The presence of this hardcoded endpoint in both the CLI entrypoint (dist/bin.js) and a separate dist chunk indicates it is reachable from the package's main code paths, not isolated test fixtures. This matches the unambiguous attack-fingerprint pattern of a hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 bound to fetch/POST in shipped distribution code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
